items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Ha... this place always brings me peace.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: You want to know who I really am so you can connect the dots on that incident, right?

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: I can tell you... but only if you promise me one thing: Let me stay here. At the Academy. As a student.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Is it really so important to you?

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: ...Yeah. More than I can say.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: I'll take your silence as a yes then.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: So, you've heard those old "pulling a switcheroo" stories, right?
HQ... this is Lynae...
Sorry Overseer... we were... ambushed...
Don't worry... I'll make it to Startorch Academy in Lahai-Roi in time...
I'll complete the mission... assigned by the Grand Architect...

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Spoiler alert: their mission wasn't completed.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Professor Warren knows I'm from the New Federation, but not exactly which part.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: It's called the Lawless Zone. Let's just say... you'd see blood spilled every three days, on average. That's where I grew up.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: I had talent, and the New Fed saw it. Put me to work. Off the record. Dirty jobs.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Then that day happened… It was an accident, but I saw my chance.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: ...An admission letter to Lahai-Roi's Startorch Academy.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: I found that recording I played you on the real Lynae's liaison at the Academy.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: So I quickly learned she wasn't just a regular student from the start.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Even so, I came to Lahai-Roi.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Everything here… It's nothing like the New Fed. People don't question themselves when they wake up, brush their teeth, and look in the mirror. They just know who they are.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: And you want to be one of them.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Look, as far as I'm concerned, the Fractsidus threatens my school life. They're your enemy too.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: We have shared interests. That's a solid basis for us to cooperate.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: ...A "student" wouldn't talk like that.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Even if we solve this, do you think you'll be able to look at yourself in the mirror afterward?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: That being said, I do know what matters most now.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: I stood up to Warren for you because I chose to trust you. And I'll own that choice.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Ha... you know, sometimes you really do sound like a teacher.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Alright then, let me rephrase.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: I like this school. I've met good people here, people who treat me like their friend. People worth protecting.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: That's why I hope you'll help me find out why the Fractsidus are here in Lahai-Roi, {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Let's do this. Together.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: I've been holding in these words forever. Ahh.. Feels good to finally get 'em out.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: As for our first lead...

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: What we do know is... Hyvatia's malfunction and the contaminated Exoswarm are definitely connected to this device I found.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: It looks like Academy tech... maybe the Department of Engineering's lab can shed some light.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: But why would the Academy allow something this dangerous to circulate freely?

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Maybe its appearance is just a disguise. Or maybe it means its maker had free access to the Department's resources.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: After all, that "contact" who's been feeding me orders might still be inside the Academy.

options.text: Makes sense. Then we start with the Department of Engineering.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: If you're ready to start making moves, I know a shortcut.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Right this way!

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Quick and clean.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: It's been overwritten. Don't have to worry about it being remotely controlled. Glad we pulled it off in time…

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Hah… You got me. It's been a while since I've done something like this. Gotten a bit rusty.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Stay in your comfort zone too long and you start to slack off without realizing it... Back in the day, I would've handled this in 28 seconds flat.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Because… the longest reboot time for the Auto-Defense Array is 28 seconds.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: ...You think?

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Ahem... sorry, back to the topic. While digging through the database, I noticed something.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: The Academy's mechs and the <te href=700014>Voidmatter Barrier</te> security logs show unusual access. Someone used admin-level protocols.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Also, the device's data has three frequency waveforms. One from Hyvatia. The other two are... unrecognizable.

options.text: How do you know that's Hyvatia's waveform?

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Um... Heh, just by, uh... browsing the Academy Forum.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Mm, alright, I was once tasked to hack into the Institute's database and tamper with Hyvatia's calibration data.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: But I didn't change anything. Just threw in a few emojis and left a note telling them to upgrade their encryption.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: That backfired though. Got me flagged.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Back to those waveforms… If one is Hyvatia's, then the other two are likely the targets for the next experiment.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: I think you're right. Next, we need to figure out what those waveforms correspond to. Maybe we'll find a clue in the Lahai-Roi ecological studies.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Let's head to the Spacetrek Archive then.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Ahem, allow me to introduce Miss I.R.I.S. She's—

options.text: One of Startorch's Seven Great Mysteries?

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: That's right! She's the Archive's administrator, responsible for indexing all the data here. A very reliable senior, you could say.

items.speaker: I.R.I.S.
items.text: Hello, I'm I.R.I.S., the retrieval AI of the Spacetrek Archive.

items.speaker: I.R.I.S.
items.text: Miss Lynae, and… {PlayerName}, what can I help you with today?

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: You look beat. Everything okay, Miss I.R.I.S.?

items.speaker: I.R.I.S.
items.text: The Archive experienced a data leak recently, so I'm running system maintenance in the background. But don't worry, routine queries are still available.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: We want to look up info related to this waveform data.

items.speaker: I.R.I.S.
items.text: Searching now. Found 3,281 matching entries. Should I list them all?

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: That's a lot… Add these keywords: "Void Storm" and "Exoswarm Contamination."

items.speaker: I.R.I.S.
items.text: Understood. Narrowed down to 324 results.

items.speaker: I.R.I.S.
items.text: Down to 3 results now.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: A big improvement. Let's split up and review them.

items.speaker: I.R.I.S.
items.text: Locations for those files are marked. Need anything else? Maybe… some tips on your school life?

items.speaker: I.R.I.S.
items.text: Never call Mr. S.I.G.M.A. "Traffic Illuminator," unless you're up for a 10-minute, non-stop high-speed chase.

items.speaker: I.R.I.S.
items.text: ...Hmm, pity.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Whew… the Academy is safe. Thank goodness.

items.speaker: Surprised Student
items.text: It's them! They're back!

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Whoa... so many people here.

items.speaker: Reporter
items.text: So it's true. The Dark Thunderbolt has enrolled at Startorch! Can you walk us through that epic battle with the Voidworm?

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Dark... Thunderbolt?

options.text: How did that title catch on so widely?

items.speaker: Reporter
items.text: At any rate, your actions were legendary!

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Sorry, guys. I need to report the Voidworm incident to the Academy first.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Once that's done, I'll tell you everything you want to know. Promise.

items.speaker: Academic Affairs Teacher
items.text: You must know why we're here, Miss Lynae.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: I do. I'm not a Startorch student anymore. Rules are rules, and I should return to the New Federation.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: That's it, right?

items.speaker: Academic Affairs Teacher
items.text: We've reviewed everything. No matter what happened, the Academy is grateful for your bravery. Truth is, we never should've allowed our students to face danger like this.

items.speaker: Academic Affairs Teacher
items.text: Our duty is to keep you safe while you're still learning how to protect yourselves, not throw you into the fire.

items.speaker: Academic Affairs Teacher
items.text: Like you said, Miss Lynae, you're not a Startorch student.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Yeah. I know.

items.speaker: Academic Affairs Teacher
items.text: So if you want to be a student here, you'll need to pass the entrance exam.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: What?

items.speaker: Academic Affairs Teacher
items.text: Of course, you'll need to enroll under your real identity this time.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: That means... you're coming back to the Academy.

items.speaker: Academic Affairs Teacher
items.text: Indeed. But we must conduct another inquiry before admission.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: An inquiry? Sure. And the entrance exam? No problem.

items.speaker: Academic Affairs Teacher
items.text: Very well, Miss Lynae. Please follow me.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Thank you.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Yeah, see you!

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: Gosh. Never thought I'd be this happy to take a freaking exam.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: The Academy convenience store's specials are really good.

items.speaker: Lynae
items.text: When I become a "freshman" at Startorch, let's go grab a bite together, okay?
...Protect the sun.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (Lynae... did she really leave?)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (Either way, stopping this from getting worse is all that matters now. She said the Fractsidus wouldn't count on only one device.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (If they can control the lure remotely, then whoever's guiding the Voidworm probably isn't even on campus.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (It's unrealistic to try to hunt them down in this chaos. Luring the Voidworm away is still the best option.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (It already absorbed my frequency once. Maybe I can get on its wavelength and draw its attention.)

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: It's me, {PlayerName}. Dr. Herssen filled me in on the current situation—

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Sorry, Professor Mornye, no time. I need the location of the Institute's anti-Voidworm defenses. I'm going to lure it there.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: How did you know—

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: …Understood. Sending coordinates to your Terminal now. But listen, most of the Institute's systems are down. The Void Storm scrambled remote access across multiple sites. Restarting them will take time.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Now, the only way to activate the defense arrays is on-site. Manually.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Got it. I'll find a way to draw the Voidworm there.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: There are three arrays in total. Once you've calibrated all of them, they'll sync and trigger the Voidworm's containment.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Noted.

items.speaker: Mornye
items.text: Please... be careful.
